title: The Mom I Thought I'd Be
triggers: not the parent I wanted to be

story.opening: <break time="400ms"/>I had this whole picture of the mom I'd be. <break time="350ms"/>Turns out she doesn't exist.
story.body: <break time="350ms"/>I was going to be patient. <break time="300ms"/>Creative. <break time="250ms"/>Fun. <break time="400ms"/>Organic snacks. <break time="350ms"/>Screen-free childhood. <break time="300ms"/>Crafts on rainy days. <break time="500ms"/>I was going to be the mom in the commercials. <break time="400ms"/>Laughing while my kids made messes. <break time="350ms"/>Never raising my voice. <break time="500ms"/>Then I actually had kids. <break time="400ms"/>And I yell. <break time="350ms"/>And there's so much screen time. <break time="300ms"/>And sometimes dinner is cereal. <break time="250ms"/>And I've never once laughed while cleaning up a mess.
story.reflection: <break time="500ms"/>The mom in my head was a fantasy. <break time="400ms"/>Made up by people selling things. <break time="350ms"/>Made up by social media highlight reels. <break time="500ms"/>The real mom - <break time="400ms"/>the one who's tired and tries anyway - <break time="350ms"/>she's the one my kids actually have. <break time="300ms"/>And she's enough. <break time="500ms"/>Even when she doesn't feel like it.

when_to_share: When someone is comparing themselves to an ideal parent or feeling like they're failing
